name: detail-view
title: Detail view — read existing state + render sections
description: Pattern for rendering a detail view of a single resource (Profile, Appointment, Assessment result). Distinct from a list-detail navigation pattern: this pattern owns the composition of card sections, edit-affordance entry points, and loading/empty states for ONE resource. Pure pattern entry — no component source.

cena.whenToUse: Rendering a single resource's full view, typically reached via a list-row click or a deep link. Primary use cases: Profile/preferences detail, Appointment detail, Assessment result detail.

loadBearingProperties.name: sectionGrouping
loadBearingProperties.guidance: Default to multi-card — each conceptual group (personal info, dietary preferences, communication preferences) gets its own card. Tabs only when the view has >5 sections and tab-navigation aids comprehension.

loadBearingProperties.name: editAffordance
values: edit-route (navigate to a dedicated edit screen)
loadBearingProperties.guidance: For Profile: edit-card-mode (z-card-action button toggles the card between view + edit modes). Inline-edit is too aggressive for low-tech-literacy patients; edit-route adds a navigation hop for trivial changes.

loadBearingProperties.name: loadingState
loadBearingProperties.guidance: Skeleton-cards on first load (no previous state); previous-state-with-overlay on refresh (user has context already). Avoid spinner-overlay — it masks the content the user is here to read.

gotchas: Each card section should bind to its own slice of the resource (not the whole resource) — keeps card components focused and makes optimistic updates per-section cleaner
Edit mode toggles per-card, not per-page. Two cards in edit at once is a real UX, and the pattern supports it naturally.
Validation runs at save-time; the form-batch primitives carry validation via z-form-control's errorMessage/helpText inputs

preferenceLedgerRows.name: control-flow @if/@for blocks
preferenceLedgerRows.applies: template uses @if for loading and edit-mode toggles

preferenceLedgerRows.name: Data Connect contract shape
preferenceLedgerRows.applies: profile binds to getProfile() + getDietaryPreferences(); save binds to updatePreferences() — see data-connect-binding (deferred)

references: Andrey's anchor: cena-health-spark/patients/src/app/profile/{profile,food-preferences-edit,personal-info-card}.component.ts (real Profile implementation in his tree)
In-catalog cross-references: card (section primitive), form (edit mode), input (form controls), button (action affordance)
cena.ownership: Cena-authored 2026-05-28 — no upstream lineage; references Andrey's existing implementation as the shape anchor
